_schema: Breakdown interruption: your machine died mid-order, this is the call to Roy's shop. 6-beat structure; Mechanic self-fix check with both branches as content; all outcomes feed county memory flags. Text is scratch placeholder awaiting Director wording (law 6). Blanks B1-B3d.

intro.speaker: roy
intro.text: [BLANK B1 — Roy answering the shop phone. He already knows which machine it is before you finish the sentence. Awaiting Director.]

options.text: [BLANK B2a — Mechanic: you'll fix it yourself, you just need the part held. Awaiting Director.]
options.label: [Mechanic 40]

options.text: [BLANK B2b — send somebody out ($150). Awaiting Director.]
[BLANK B2c — it sits till you can deal with it (order loses 2 days, weather keeps rolling). Awaiting Director.]

fixed_self.speaker: roy
fixed_self.text: [BLANK B3a — Roy hears it running over the phone. Professional respect, grudging: he just lost a service call. Awaiting Director.]

bodged_it.speaker: roy
bodged_it.text: [BLANK B3b — good failure: your fix held forty minutes, now it needs the part AND the labor. Roy is professionally sympathetic and privately delighted. Parts run $80. Awaiting Director.]

options.text: (Pay for the parts and get back to it)

shop_fix.speaker: roy
shop_fix.text: [BLANK B3c — Roy confirms he'll send his guy out; friendly, efficient, and you can hear him writing the invoice. Awaiting Director.]

it_sits.speaker: roy
it_sits.text: [BLANK B3d — Roy's dry warning about machines that sit in a field with weather coming. No sale, no hard feelings, one needle. Awaiting Director.]
